previous_findings_status.detail: Links were re-pointed from github.com/marketplace/rafayels-engineering to github.com/rgardishyan/rafayels-engineering — but that repo also returns HTTP 404. All 5 external links (agents, View on GitHub, GitHub footer, Documentation, Issues, Changelog) are STILL broken. The fix was cosmetic (label changes) not functional. This is now worse because the label says 'View on GitHub' implying a real repo, creating false confidence.

previous_findings_status.finding: Footer links aliased to same URL (P1)
previous_findings_status.detail: Issues → /issues, Changelog → /releases, Documentation → #readme. Distinct paths now used. (URLs themselves still 404, but the aliasing issue is resolved.)

previous_findings_status.finding: Generic SaaS template, no distinctive element (P1)
previous_findings_status.detail: SVG orbit illustration added to hero provides some visual identity. Colored pipeline steps and agent badges add rhythm. But overall structure remains hero→pipeline→features→agents→CTA→footer — the exact template pattern flagged.
Step numbers now use display-sized colored numerals. Pipeline icons show accent colors by default. Visual hierarchy is stronger, though cards still feel underpopulated.
Pipeline steps and feature icons now use multi-color palette (violet, emerald, orange, cyan). The light theme is still muted overall but no longer monochromatic blue.
Per philosophy.md, serif is now used consistently across hero, section titles, and card headings — creating more editorial cohesion. Gap narrowed but not closed.

previous_findings_status.finding: No copy-to-clipboard feedback, 'How It Works' is static (P2)

previous_findings_status.detail: Icons enlarged from 40×40 to 52×52px with 26px SVGs. Now recognizable at reading distance.
Nav now renders immediately with transparent background, confirmed in iteration-2 screenshots.

previous_findings_status.finding: Dense agent grid, minimal badge differentiation (P3)
previous_findings_status.detail: Agent type badges now have colored pill styling. Still dense but more scannable.
Theme toggle added in nav, persists to localStorage per philosophy.md.

new_or_worsened_findings.description: ALL 5 external links still return HTTP 404. The repo github.com/rgardishyan/rafayels-engineering does not exist. Verified via curl: main repo 404, /issues 404, /releases 404. The improver changed the URL and label but never verified the target resolves. This is now MORE deceptive because 'View on GitHub' implies a real repo.
new_or_worsened_findings.recommendation: NEVER: change a broken link to a different broken link without verifying resolution. INSTEAD: use `curl -sI <url>` to confirm HTTP 200 before committing. If the repo is private or not yet created, use a placeholder like '#coming-soon' with a visible badge, or remove the link entirely.

new_or_worsened_findings.description: The terminal demo block (screenshot-2, scrollY ~1600px) shows a single command 're feature "Add user authentication"' in a large empty white card. The vast whitespace-to-content ratio makes it look like a rendering error or unfinished section. No output preview, no animation, no context.
new_or_worsened_findings.recommendation: NEVER: show a single line of input in a full-width terminal block with no simulated output. INSTEAD: add a typewriter animation with simulated output lines (agent spawning, review pass, PR link) to demonstrate the workflow in action.

new_or_worsened_findings.description: In light mode (all screenshots), the hero SVG orbit illustration is extremely faint — pale blue circles on near-white background. The illustration that's supposed to be the distinctive visual element is nearly invisible. The segmentation map (screenshot-0-segmentation) confirms the SVG elements are present but they have virtually no contrast in light theme.
new_or_worsened_findings.recommendation: NEVER: design a signature illustration only for dark mode and let it wash out in light. INSTEAD: use light-mode-specific SVG fill/stroke values (e.g., --blue at 40% opacity minimum) or swap to a bolder illustration variant via the theme class.

new_or_worsened_findings.description: The 'Built for Serious Work' capabilities section (screenshot-2 → screenshot-3) has a stark visual break from the pipeline section above it. Pipeline has colored accents and visual rhythm; capabilities drops to plain white cards with small icons and dense body text. The transition feels like entering a different, blander page.
new_or_worsened_findings.recommendation: NEVER: let adjacent sections have drastically different visual energy levels. INSTEAD: carry the colored accent system into capability cards — use the colored top-border-on-hover pattern, tinted icon backgrounds, or section-level background tinting to bridge the visual gap.

new_or_worsened_findings.description: The stats bar in the hero ('29 agents, 22 skills, 19 commands, 16 hooks') is present per philosophy.md but renders as tiny, low-contrast text that doesn't register as social proof. At screenshot scale the numbers are barely readable.
new_or_worsened_findings.recommendation: NEVER: bury quantitative social proof in body-sized muted text. INSTEAD: use display-sized numerals (var(--text-xl)) with accent color, paired with smaller labels, in a distinct horizontal band or card row.

fixes_failed: external links still 404 — repo URL doesn't exist
improvement_summary.net_direction: mixed — visual polish improved, UX regressed due to persistent P0

summary: Iteration 1's changes delivered meaningful visual improvements: nav is visible on load, theme toggle works, pipeline steps have colored hierarchy, feature icons are properly sized, and the multi-color palette reduces the monochromatic feel. However, the CRITICAL P0 finding (broken external links) was NOT actually fixed — URLs were changed from marketplace to repo paths, but the repo github.com/rgardishyan/rafayels-engineering returns 404. All 5 external links remain dead. Three new P2 findings surfaced: the hero SVG orbit illustration washes out in light mode, the terminal demo block looks empty/broken with a single command line, and the capabilities section has a jarring visual energy drop from the pipeline above. UX score drops 3 points due to the false-fix on links creating worse user deception. Overall subjective average: 66.75 (was 65.5) — marginal net gain, blocked by the P0.
